paragraphs.text: ABOUT NEITHER BEING OBDURATE 218. HOW ABBA SERGIOS PACIFIED A CURSING NOR REMAINING OBDURATE FARMER BY PATIENCE Once when 1 was in the Holy City a person who loved Christ came The higoumen of the monastery of Abba Constantine, Abba to me and said: ‘There had been a small altercation between my Sergios, told us: brother and me and he will not be reconciled with me. You go Once we were travelling with a holy elder and we lost our way. speak to him and reason with him’. I received this commission Quite without meaning to, and indeed without knowing where we joyfully. I called the brother and spoke to him of those things which were going, we found ourselves in sowed fields and we trod down tend to love and peace, and it seemed as though be was coming some of the seedlings. The farmer was working there and he noticed round to my point of view. At Jast, he said to me: I cannot be what we had done. He began to upbraid us angrily in these words: reconciled with him because 1 swore on the cross’. I said to him “You are monks? You fear God? If you had the fear of God before with a smile: Your cath was equivalent to saying: “Oh Christ, by your eyes you would not have done this.” At once the holy elder

194 The Spiritual Meadow John Mosclios 195 said 10 us: ‘For the Lord's sake, let nobody say anything’, and he chalice that I had no knowledge of having done such a thing, but addressed the farmer: ‘Well spoken, my child. If we had the fear of he was not convinced. Then I became myself again and thought of God, we would not be doing these things." Again the farmer spoke these words of the holy fathers <that each man should always angry and abusive words, to which the elder again responded: You question himseif on every matter>. 1 put my trust in them and speak the truth, child, when you say that if we were true monks we changed my line of reasoning a little. I said to myself: “The kindly would not have done this; but, for the sake of the Lord, forgive . deacon loves me and, prompted by his love for me, he has confided <us>, for we have sianed’, The farmer was astonished. He came and to me that which was in his heart concerning me to put me on my threw himself at the feet of the elder, saying: ‘I have sinned, forgive guard. I will make sure that 1 do not do thal deed in future. But, me, and for the Lord's sake, take me with you". The blessed Sergios oh, wretched soul! While you say you have not done that deed, are said: ‘And in truth he followed along with us; and when he came there not thousands of misdeeds done by you which you have <here> he received the monastic habit’, forgotten?
